description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped rock with a rugged, angular form and a predominantly dark gray coloration. The surface is uneven, featuring a mix of jagged edges, rough patches, and some smoother, weathered areas. Subtle striations and mineral veins are visible, suggesting natural geological processes at work. One side displays a lighter patch, possibly from a scrape or mineral deposit, while the rest of the rock maintains a consistent, stony texture. The rock appears dense and solid, with no visible cavities or openings. Its overall appearance suggests it could serve as a natural obstacle or decorative element in an urban park or landscaped area.
description: A dark gray, angular rock with a rough, uneven surface and visible mineral striations. It features jagged edges and a lighter patch on one side.
description_view_0: This view shows the rock's broadest face, highlighting its angular shape and the layered, striated texture across the surface.
description_view_1: From this angle, the rock appears more elongated, with a pointed end and a visible seam running along its length.
description_view_2: This side reveals a prominent lighter patch, possibly a mineral deposit or scrape, contrasting with the otherwise dark gray surface.
description_view_3: The rock looks narrower and more vertical, with a rugged, uneven surface and pronounced textural details.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped sidewalk edge
